violations.description: General employee practices. — --Food employee observed without proper hair restraint. -Cook with a beard was not wearing a beard net.

Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item stored in an unapproved location. -Case of sweetener packets stored below sewer line. *Corrected on-site.--Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. -2 boxes of miscellaneous items stored on floor in walk in freezer.

Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Food removed from original packaging was not placed in a container with an impervious and nonabsorbent cover. -Container of chips stored on top shelf above prep table without lid. *Minimal left, staff voluntarily disposed of product.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food was not consumed/discarded within 10 calendar days from the date of preparation. -Murtha sauce dated 5/17. *Staff voluntarily disposed of product.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Debris at blade of can opener. -Build up of limescale and black debris at interior upper surface of ice machine. -Black debris along bottom back edge of ice chute at soda machine.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Spill/debris at door edges of meat prep table. -Dust/debris at fan covers in beer walk in cooler.

Plumbing system. — --Plumbing system was not designed, constructed, operated and/or maintained according to regulation. -2 buckets in basement to catch leaks below plumbing lines. -Hose with spray nozzle left connected to (upper kitchen) mop sink faucet without back flow protection in place.

Handwashing lavatory supplies. — --Handwash lavatory was not supplied with supply of individual disposable towels, continuous towel supply system, or heated air-drying device. -Main Kitchen hand sink out of paper towels upon arrival. *COS-directed to stock immediately.

Food supplies. — --Food item was adulterated, contaminated or unsafe for human consumption. -Multiple items out of temperature in walk in cooler: 2 bloated bags of raw chicken wings, 3 open and moldy containers of vegetable oil whipped spread, Blue butter 46F (made 12/10-misdated as 11/10?); discolored pattied raw hamburgers (made 12/9?-misdated as 11/9?) Whole bags of guacamole + one in-use bag at salad table all have use by date of Jul 31, 2025, and chili at 45F. -2 door glass cooler had Blue Butter with date of 10/25 and Murthas sauce with date of 10/15. *All disposed of while on-site.

General food protection. — --Food item was not protected from potential contamination. -Cases and bus tubs of food in walk in freezer have "caught" water from drain pan and and have a large amount of ice build up.--Potentially hazardous food was not stored at proper temperature of 41F or less. *Walk in cooler had been set to 45F by refrigeration tech since tap lines had been freezing, done approximately one week ago. *Unit turned down while on-site and whole packed foods moved to colder refrigeration until unit recovers to 41F or less.

Refrigerated Storage. — --Refrigerator or cold storage was insufficient or ineffective at maintaining potentially hazardous food at a temperature of 41F or less. -Walk in cooler had been set to 45F by refrigeration tech since tap lines had been freezing, done approximately one week ago. *Unit turned down while on-site and whole packed foods moved to colder refrigeration until unit recovers to 41F or less. -Dessert slices at glass door storage side of beer cooler at ~45F. *Moved to glass door condiment refrigerator.--Walk in freezer fan's drain pan has overflowed water, created a layer of ice in walk in freezer.

Variance requirement for specialized processing methods. — --8 or more black tubs work of smoked meats and foods from Brews and BBQ kitchen stored in walk in freezer with dates from August 2025. *In sealed bags but could not verify if they were vacuum packed.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned adequately. -Staining at cutting boards at hot hold, kitchen left prep table and server salad prep table. -Multiple knives at knife block above hand sink have dried food debris/residue on blades.--Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Food debris at interior of cookline (2 door) prep table. -Food debris at can opener blade and surfaces.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Walk in cooler has food spills and debris build up throughout unit: pans, food tubs, shelves, fans, and floor. -Residue at handles of cook line (2 door) prep table. -Heavy build up of debris at handle and interior of unused oven at cookline. -Residue at front surface of microwave. -Debris/residue at interior surfaces of 2 door glass cooler. -Utility carts have crumb/debris/residue build up.--Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. -Heavy grease/debris build up at hood vents.
Manual cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Employee observed washing hands in a ware wash sink. -Employee washed hands at rinse sink of 3 compartment sink.

Sanitizing solution testing devices. — --Test strip or kit that accurately measures the sanitizing concentration was not provided. -Facility does not have the correct test strips for the sink and surface sanitizer.
Mechanical cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Ware wash machine was not maintained and/or operated as required. -Dish washer has a build up of limescale/clogged sprayers.
Employee dressing areas and lockers. — ---Open bottle of liquor and cans of sprite at top shelves in walk in cooler.

General premises. — --Food establishment and all parts of the property in connection with their operation were not kept free from litter. -Dry storage at back area near kitchen exits is cluttered with empty boxes, grease pan and misc. items.
Storage and handling of maintenance items. — ---Hand sink at bar used as dump sink-Customer drink glass ice dumped into sink.
Clean dry location, protected (1 pt deducted)
Proper storage, no-reuse (1 pt deducted)
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Proper storage, labeling, use (3 pts deducted)

Proper storage, no-reuse (1 pt deducted)
Proper storage, labeling, use (3 pts deducted)
Clean/dry location, protected, covered, original containers, labeled (2 pts deducted)
Prevented, separated, segregated (5 pts deducted)
